sentence: Sarah Okonkwo finally finished reading a book she had started over a year ago.
Among the things James Ferreira cannot explain is why he always wakes up just before his alarm goes off.
The older Daniel Mwangi gets, the more he appreciates a quiet evening at home.
Elena Vasquez has been putting off a difficult conversation for longer than she cares to admit.
Without even realizing it, Omar Al-Rashid has developed a very specific way of loading the dishwasher.
Priya Nair spent a good ten minutes this week looking for something that was in her hand the whole time.
There is a song that, whenever Tom Brannigan hears it, takes him straight back to a particular moment in his life.
Sophie Lindqvist has been genuinely unsure lately whether she is tired because of stress or just not drinking enough water.
Carlos Mendes keeps telling himself he will reorganize his desk, and keeps not doing it.
It occurred to Rachel Zimmerman recently that she has no memory of learning to ride a bike, yet she can still do it perfectly.
A checkout worker was unusually kind to Marcus Webb last Tuesday, and he is still thinking about it.
Fatima Al-Amin has a drawer somewhere in her home that she avoids opening because of how chaotic it has become.
Whenever Luca Bianchi has to parallel park with people watching, everything he knows about driving temporarily leaves his body.
Anika Johansson recently came across a photo of herself from ten years ago and felt a complicated mix of emotions.
Something about the smell of rain hit David Osei differently last week in a way he couldn't quite put into words.
Nina Castellano has strong opinions about the correct way to make tea, which not everyone appreciates.
The version of tomorrow that Ben Hargreaves imagined in his head was considerably more productive than the one that actually happened.
Grace Tanaka has a habit of narrating small tasks to herself under her breath without noticing she is doing it.
Somewhere in Samuel Dupont's phone is a note he wrote to himself at 2am that makes no sense to him now.
After years of swearing it would never happen, Rita Ogundimu has become her mother in at least three distinct and undeniable ways.
